howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Hospet
topPlaces.description: Hospet (officially Hosapete) is the largest and fastest-growing industrial city and district headquarters of the Vijayanagara district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Hospet is known as "the steel city of Karnataka".

topPlaces.name: Ramasagara
topPlaces.description: Ramasagara is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Danapuram
topPlaces.description: Dhanapura is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Malapanagudi
topPlaces.description: Malapanagudi is an ancient fortified village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Devasamudra
topPlaces.description: Devasamudra is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Kamalapura, Vijayanagara
topPlaces.description: Kamalapura (also Kamalapur and Kamalapuram) is a panchayat town in Vijayanagara district in Karnataka. It is located near the industrial city of Hosapete, and lies within the ruins of the old city of Vijayanagara, just outside the original 'Royal Centre'.

topPlaces.name: Tungabhadra Dam
topPlaces.description: The Tungabhadra Dam, also known as Pampa Sagar, is a water reservoir constructed across the Tungabhadra River in the Hosapete-Koppal confluence in Karnataka, India. It is a multipurpose dam serving irrigation, electricity generation, flood control, etc.

topPlaces.name: Lotus Mahal
topPlaces.description: Lotus Mahal or Chitrangini Mahal is an important secular structure in Hampi, India. The Lotus Mahal is an example of Vijayanagara style of architecture and is a two storied structure built in Rubble masonry and finely plastered.

items.note: Hospet (officially Hosapete) is the largest and fastest-growing industrial city and district headquarters of the Vijayanagara district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Hospet is known as "the steel city of Karnataka".
Ramasagara is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.
Dhanapura is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.
Malapanagudi is an ancient fortified village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.
Devasamudra is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hospet taluk of Bellary district in Karnataka.

Kamalapura (also Kamalapur and Kamalapuram) is a panchayat town in Vijayanagara district in Karnataka. It is located near the industrial city of Hosapete, and lies within the ruins of the old city of Vijayanagara, just outside the original 'Royal Centre'.

The Tungabhadra Dam, also known as Pampa Sagar, is a water reservoir constructed across the Tungabhadra River in the Hosapete-Koppal confluence in Karnataka, India. It is a multipurpose dam serving irrigation, electricity generation, flood control, etc.

Lotus Mahal or Chitrangini Mahal is an important secular structure in Hampi, India. The Lotus Mahal is an example of Vijayanagara style of architecture and is a two storied structure built in Rubble masonry and finely plastered.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ananthasayana temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ananthasayana temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ananthasayana temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (137 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ananthasayana temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ananthasayana temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ananthasayana temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ananthasayana temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ananthasayana temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hospet, Ramasagara, Danapuram, Malapanagudi, Devasamudra.
